rationale: RocksDB uses separate thread pools for background maintenance work (compaction, memtable flush, file purge) vs. user-facing read/write operations. Background threads are named 'rocksdb:<priority>' via pthread_setname_np in util/threadpool_imp.cc (line 394), where priority is 'low' (compaction), 'high' (flush/purge), or 'bottom' (bottom-level compaction). All background threads share the 8-byte prefix 'rocksdb:'. User/application threads (e.g., db_bench read/write workers) do not carry this prefix. Under CPU oversubscription, compaction and flush threads compete with foreground query threads for CPU time, causing tail latency spikes. By deprioritizing all 'rocksdb:' prefixed threads, the scheduler ensures foreground operations get immediate CPU access while background maintenance work is deferred, reducing P99/P99.9 latency without starving compaction.

threads.purpose: LOW priority thread pool: executes LSM-tree compaction (BGWorkCompaction) merging SST files across levels, and overflow memtable flushes when the HIGH pool is saturated. This is the primary CPU-intensive background task.

HIGH priority thread pool: executes memtable flush (BGWorkFlush) writing in-memory data to SST files on disk, and obsolete file purge (BGWorkPurge) deleting old SST/WAL files. Flush is higher priority than compaction but still a background I/O-heavy operation.

BOTTOM priority thread pool: executes bottom-level compaction (BGWorkBottomCompaction) for the largest LSM-tree level. This is the lowest priority and most CPU-intensive compaction work, intentionally separated to avoid blocking higher-priority compactions.

Backup engine thread: only active during database backup operations (BackupEngine). Sets comm to 'backup_engine' via pthread_setname_np in utilities/backup/backup_engine.cc. Rarely present during normal operation.
